title: Fact-resolution decision
description: The immutable authorization target for one human fact-resolution (DEC-0016): the COMPLETE requested action, prepared deterministically from Artifact Store references — never from caller-supplied contradiction arrays. It binds one currently open contradiction of one validated truth analysis to exactly one winning claim and every other participating claim as a losing claim (exact partition; partial resolution is prohibited), and it pins the exact state the human decided on: content digests of the analysis, snapshot, profile, and every participating claim, plus the snapshot's aggregate claim-set digest — so a stale analysis, stale snapshot, winner substitution, loser omission or injection, wrong fact slot, cross-brand substitution, lineage-head change, or reordered/duplicated reference is detectable after signing. The Product Owner signs this artifact's exact store address and recomputed content digest under the fact-resolution-approval gate (DEC-0014/DEC-0015); signing a truth analysis, a bare fact key, or winner/loser IDs without this envelope is prohibited. Contract validity proves shape and internal consistency only — application additionally requires verified, consumed approval evidence over this exact artifact. The contradiction fingerprint is deterministic (contradiction-fingerprint-sha256-1.0.0: 'c' + sha256 hex over the canonical JSON of {brand_ref, claim_refs, distinct_values, fact_key, workspace}; semantic layer recomputes).

workspace.description: workspace namespace the decision belongs to; together with brand_ref it pins the exact namespace — every referenced artifact lives in this workspace/brand, and cross-brand or cross-workspace substitution fails closed

fingerprint_algorithm.description: versioned fingerprint algorithm; a future change is a new version, never a silent reinterpretation

truth_analysis_ref.description: artifact_id of the truth analysis the contradiction was selected from

truth_analysis_digest.description: canonical content digest of the exact analysis artifact at preparation time (claim-set-sha256-1.0.0 content digest)

claim_snapshot_ref.description: artifact_id of the claim snapshot the analysis is bound to

claim_snapshot_digest.description: canonical content digest of the exact snapshot artifact at preparation time

claim_set_digest.description: the snapshot's aggregate claim-set digest: binds the COMPLETE canonical namespace contents at preparation time, so any later claim appearance, disappearance, content change, or lineage-head change makes the decision detectably stale (DEC-0013)

fact_key.description: the single-cardinality fact slot whose open contradiction this decision resolves

contradiction_fingerprint.description: deterministic identity of the selected contradiction; recomputed by the semantic layer over {brand_ref, claim_refs, distinct_values, fact_key, workspace} — a fabricated fingerprint cannot claim a different contradiction than the recorded one

contradiction.description: the exact contradiction being resolved, copied verbatim from the referenced analysis at preparation time; the partition and fingerprint checks run against these recorded values

claim_refs.description: every participating claim, sorted in code-unit order (semantic layer)

winning_claim_ref.description: exactly one participating claim the human selects as prevailing; the winner is NOT mutated, upgraded to verified, or re-issued by application

winning_claim_digest.description: canonical content digest of the winning claim at preparation time; winner substitution after signing is detectable

losing_claims.description: every participating claim other than the winner, exactly once, sorted by claim_ref in code-unit order (semantic layer); winner + losers must partition contradiction.claim_refs exactly (semantic layer)

rationale.description: the requester's stated reason for the proposed resolution; recorded verbatim in the signed envelope

requester_id.description: identity that requested decision preparation; the approval evidence carries its own signed requester_id and self_review declaration (DEC-0008)

contradiction.description: single-cardinality fact slot 'identity.primary_name' carries 2 distinct normalized values across 2 eligible claims; resolution requires a human decision

examples.rationale: The client's registration certificate and profile cover both print the shorter name; the longer form appears once in an outdated brochure.
